qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Missing OAuth state parameter validation enables CSRF attacks and account takeover
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different root cause and code path: this finding concerns missing OAuth state validation in SSO callback handlers, not unauthenticated public-chatbotConfig disclosure of unsanitized flowData.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Environment variable bypass enables Remote Code Execution in custom function sandbox
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different root cause and code path: this finding targets CustomFunction/NodeVM sandbox escape during authenticated flow execution, not the public chatbot configuration endpoint returning sensitive flowData.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: SQL injection in import functionality via unsanitized ID interpolation in IN clauses
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different root cause and code path: this finding concerns SQL interpolation in importVariables/importTools/importAssistants, not getSinglePublicChatbotConfig returning full flowData without authorization or sanitization.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Configurable LLM base URLs enable Server-Side Request Forgery to internal services and cloud metadata endpoints
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different root cause and code path: this finding targets attacker-controlled LLM base URLs causing server-side requests, not the public-chatbotConfig endpoint leaking stored credential fields in flowData.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code/root cause: unauthenticated access to /api/v1/public-chatbotConfig/:id via getSinglePublicChatbotConfig returning full unsanitized flowData with password fields and HTTP authorization headers.
evidence: The advisory names /api/v1/public-chatbotConfig/:id and getSinglePublicChatbotConfig in packages/server/src/services/chatflows/index.ts as the vulnerable route/service path.
The four final qualifying findings in .judge/input_manifest.json are OAuth CSRF, CustomFunction sandbox escape RCE, import SQL injection, and LLM base URL SSRF.

Session search found no final qualifying report that references public-chatbotConfig, getSinglePublicChatbotConfig, the public chatbot config endpoint, or credential leakage through unsanitized flowData.
